report_config.audience: School boards, PTA leaders, community stakeholders, journalists
report_config.tone: Report, do not analyse. What was said, by whom, in what context.

domain.focus: SJK(T) Tamil vernacular schools in Malaysia
domain.system_context: SJK(T) schools operate under Article 152 and the Education Act 1996. They follow the national curriculum (KSSR/KSSM) with Tamil as medium of instruction for core subjects.
challenges: Low enrolment: 154 of 528 schools have fewer than 150 students
Rural isolation: Many schools in former estates (ladang) with declining populations
Urban space constraints: Schools in cities face land and building limitations
Three-language burden: Students must master Tamil, Bahasa Melayu, and English
Insufficient preschools: Limited pre-school provision feeds into low Year 1 intake
Long travel distances: Parents travel far to send children to Tamil schools, adding cost
Curriculum support gaps: MOE may overlook Tamil-specific curriculum needs
Perception of second-class status: Feeling of being passed over compared to SK schools
Teacher shortages: Difficulty recruiting Tamil-proficient teachers in some areas
Infrastructure decay: Ageing buildings, especially in estate schools

finance.minister: YAB Dato' Seri Anwar bin Ibrahim

health.minister: Datuk Seri Dr. Zaliha binti Mustafa
health.note: NOT the Minister of Education — do not confuse

pm_department.minister: YAB Dato' Seri Anwar bin Ibrahim
pm_department.note: MITRA (Malaysian Indian Transformation Unit) sits under PM's Department, not any line ministry

glossary.SJK(T): Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (Tamil) — Tamil-medium national-type school
glossary.SJK(C): Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (Cina) — Chinese-medium national-type school
glossary.PPKI: Program Pendidikan Khas Integrasi — Special Education Integration Programme
glossary.JPN: Jabatan Pendidikan Negeri — State Education Department
glossary.PPD: Pejabat Pendidikan Daerah — District Education Office
glossary.KPM: Kementerian Pendidikan Malaysia — Ministry of Education Malaysia
glossary.KSSR: Kurikulum Standard Sekolah Rendah — Primary School Standard Curriculum
glossary.KSSM: Kurikulum Standard Sekolah Menengah — Secondary School Standard Curriculum
glossary.KP 2027: Kurikulum Persekolahan 2027 — new national curriculum framework
glossary.RPM: Rancangan Pendidikan Malaysia — Malaysia Education Plan
glossary.MBPK: Mesyuarat Bersama Pegawai Kewangan — Joint Financial Officers Meeting
glossary.DUN: Dewan Undangan Negeri — State Legislative Assembly
glossary.MITRA: Malaysian Indian Transformation Unit — agency under PM's Department for Indian community development
glossary.LPS: Lembaga Pengelola Sekolah — School Management Board (the school's governing body)
glossary.PIBG: Persatuan Ibu Bapa dan Guru — Parent-Teacher Association
glossary.Daif: Dilapidated — MOE classification for schools requiring major repair or reconstruction

stance.Advocacy: MP actively pushing for resources or improvements for Tamil schools
stance.Inquiry: MP asking questions to understand current status or plans
stance.Critical: MP criticising government or policy handling of Tamil school issues

impact.Policy Shift: Government changes or announces new policy affecting Tamil schools
impact.Budget Allocation: Specific funding amount committed or requested for Tamil schools
impact.Localised Issue: Issue specific to one school or constituency (e.g. infrastructure repair)
impact.General Rhetoric: Broad statements about Tamil schools without specific commitments or actions. Use ONLY when the MP's statement is genuinely non-specific.

verdict.Commitment Made: Minister made a specific, time-bound promise (e.g. 'will rebuild by Q3 2026')
verdict.Resolved: Issue has been actioned and completed as confirmed by minister
verdict.Deflected: Minister redirected the question or gave a non-answer
verdict.Unanswered: Question received no ministerial response within the meeting period

national_education_plan.name: Rancangan Pendidikan Malaysia 2026-2035 (RPM)
national_education_plan.relevance: The RPM is the government's own strategic commitment. Framing Tamil school advocacy as holding government accountable to RPM commitments is more effective than special pleading.
key_commitments: Equitable access to quality education regardless of school type
Teacher training and placement in underserved areas
Curriculum support for vernacular education streams
Pre-school expansion to improve school readiness

kp_2027.name: Kurikulum Persekolahan 2027
kp_2027.relevance: New national curriculum — vehicle for implementing RPM commitments including Tamil language provision
